Notes: Enough for the author to pick 3–5 winners

What pass requires: Each fact would plausibly make a 10-year-old say "wait; seriously?!"
Notes: The bar is genuine surprise; not importance

What pass requires: No specific commercial brand names appear — descriptive categories used instead ("a popular cosmetic brand")
Notes: Children's books avoid free advertising and dated references

What pass requires: No fact reads like an award citation or resume line ("won the Padma Bhushan"; "directed 5 films")
Notes: Honors are impressive to adults; invisible to kids

What pass requires: Each fact contains a specific; picturable detail — nothing like "she was a pioneer"
Notes: Specificity is what makes facts stick

What pass requires: Each candidate traces back to a sourced fact from the brief
Notes: Did You Know facts get quoted by kids — they must be true
